name: Generate Clean Plates with Seedance and Automate Object Removal, QC, and Delivery

parameters.jsCode:
const d = $input.first().json;

const nukeScript = `# Nuke Script – AI Clean Plate Comp Template
# Shot: ${d.shotCode} | Sequence: ${d.sequenceCode}
# Object Removal: ${d.objectType} | Pass: ${d.passType}
# QC Score: ${d.qcScore} | QC Passed: ${d.qcPassed}
# Auto-generated by Clean Plate Generator workflow
# Date: ${new Date().toISOString()}

set cut_paste_input [stack 0]
version 15.0

# ─── ORIGINAL PLATE ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Read {
 file "${d.plateImageUrl}"
 format "1920 1080 0 0 1920 1080 1 HD_1080"
 name Read_ORIGINAL_PLATE
 label "Original Plate"
 xpos 0
 ypos -400
}

# ─── AI CLEAN PLATE ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Read {
 file "${d.videoUrl}"
 format "1920 1080 0 0 1920 1080 1 HD_1080"
 name Read_AI_CLEAN_PLATE
 label "AI Clean – ${d.label}"
 xpos 200
 ypos -400
}

# ─── DIFFERENCE MAP ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Merge2 {
 inputs 2
 operation difference
 name Merge_DIFF_MAP
 label "Difference Map"
 xpos 100
 ypos -300
}

Grade {
 blackpoint 0
 whitepoint 0.05
 name Grade_DIFF_BOOST
 label "Boost Difference"
 xpos 100
 ypos -240
}

# ─── MAIN COMP MERGE ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Merge2 {
 inputs 2
 operation over
 name Merge_CLEAN_OVER_PLATE
 label "Clean over Original"
 xpos 100
 ypos -100
}

# ─── GRADE MATCH ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ColorCorrect {
 name CC_Grade_Match
 label "Grade Match to Plate"
 xpos 100
 ypos 0
}

# ─── WRITE NODES ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Write {
 file "/mnt/delivery/${d.shotCode}/clean_plate_${d.passType}_v001.####.exr"
 file_type exr
 colorspace linear
 name Write_CLEAN_PLATE
 label "Output Clean Plate"
 xpos 0
 ypos 100
}

Write {
 file "/mnt/delivery/${d.shotCode}/diff_map_${d.passType}_v001.####.exr"
 file_type exr
 colorspace linear
 name Write_DIFF_MAP
 label "Output Diff Map"
 xpos 200
 ypos 100
}
`;

return [{ json: {
  ...d,
  nukeScript:     nukeScript,
  nukeScriptName: `${d.shotCode}_clean_${d.passType}_v001.nk`,
  nukeScriptPath: `/comp_templates/${d.shotCode}/${d.shotCode}_clean_${d.passType}_v001.nk`,
  folderStructure: {
    cleanPlates:    `/delivery/${d.shotCode}/clean_plates/`,
    differenceMaps: `/delivery/${d.shotCode}/diff_maps/`,
    nukeScripts:    `/delivery/${d.shotCode}/nuke_scripts/`
  }
} }];

parameters.name: ={{ $('Generate Nuke Comp Script').first().json.shotCode }}_{{ $('Generate Nuke Comp Script').first().json.passType }}_{{ $('Generate Nuke Comp Script').first().json.variantId }}.mp4

parameters.content:
## 🎬 AI Clean Plate Generator

### How it works
This workflow automates AI-assisted clean plate generation for VFX production. When triggered via webhook, it fans out four parallel render passes — primary clean plate, an alternative take, a foreground-removal pass, and a difference map — using the Seedance AI video model with your original plate image as reference.

Each pass is polled until complete, then scored against a configurable QC threshold. Passes that clear the threshold are logged to Notion, uploaded to Google Drive, and shipped with a ready-to-use Nuke compositing script. The whole team gets notified via Slack and Telegram when results are ready.

### Setup steps
1. **Webhook** — Copy the webhook URL from the trigger node and use it in your shot management tool or pipeline script.
2. **Seedance API** — Replace the `Authorization` header value in `Seedance: Generate Clean Pass` and `Poll: Check Job Status` with your own Seedance API key stored as an n8n credential.
3. **Google Drive** — Connect a Google Drive OAuth2 credential and update the target folder ID in `Google Drive: Upload Clean Plate`.
4. **Notion** — Connect a Notion integration credential and set the correct database ID for your clean plate log.
5. **Slack & Telegram** — Connect credentials for both notification nodes and update the channel/chat IDs to match your team's setup.
6. **QC Threshold** — Send `qcThreshold` (0–1) in the webhook payload to control auto-approval sensitivity. Defaults to `0.85`.

## 📡 Trigger & Validation
Accepts incoming shot requests via POST webhook and validates required fields (`plateImageUrl`, `removalBrief`, `shotCode`). Enriches the payload with sensible defaults for optional fields like frame range, frame rate, project ID, and QC threshold before handing off downstream.

## 🎞️ Pass Generation & AI Render
Fans out into four distinct render passes and builds image-to-video requests using the plate image as reference. Each pass is submitted to the Seedance API and the returned job ID is merged with the original metadata for downstream polling.

## ⏳ Polling & Job Completion
Polls the Seedance API every 20 seconds until the render job reports `succeeded`. If still processing, execution waits and retries automatically. Once complete, metadata and QC scores are assembled for the next stage.

## 🔍 QC Check & Nuke Script
Scores each rendered pass against the configured QC threshold. Passes that meet the bar get a Nuke compositing script auto-generated with Read, Merge, Grade, and Write nodes pre-wired. Failed passes trigger an immediate artist-review alert in Slack.

## ☁️ Asset Storage & Logging
Downloads the rendered video and uploads it to Google Drive under the correct shot folder. Simultaneously logs the pass metadata — variant ID, video URL, plate URL, project ID — to a Notion database for pipeline tracking and review.

## 📣 Team Notifications
Aggregates all four passes into a single summary and sends it to both Slack and Telegram. The message includes per-pass QC scores, video links, Nuke script content, and folder paths — giving the paint and comp team everything they need at a glance.

## 🔐 Credentials & Security
Use n8n credential store for all secrets — Seedance API key, Google Drive OAuth2, Notion API, Slack OAuth2, and Telegram Bot token. Never hardcode tokens in node headers. Replace all sample IDs and folder paths with values from your own project.

## ⚠️ Error Handler
Catches any failure across the entire workflow and immediately sends a Slack alert to the ops channel. Wire this to every sub-workflow or critical node to ensure no silent failures.
